coreCompetencies: Basic relational modeling: entities, relationships and normalization to 3NF
Reads and interprets entity-relationship diagrams
Documents tables, columns and data types in a data dictionary
Distinguishes OLTP transactional workloads from OLAP analytical ones
Basic notions of star schema and fact and dimension tables
Uses diagramming tools to draft simple models
Elementary notions of a data catalog and metadata
complementaryCompetencies: Basic SQL to explore existing schemas
Familiarity with cloud storage concepts (object storage, buckets)
Awareness of data governance and privacy basics
indicators: Requires constant supervision on modeling tasks
Assists senior architects in documenting existing models
Needs 6-12 months of mentoring in data architecture

coreCompetencies: Designs normalized relational models for a bounded domain
Builds star and snowflake schemas for reporting
Documents source-to-target mappings for basic integrations
Applies naming conventions and modeling standards
Captures table-level lineage in the catalog
Handles slowly changing dimensions type 1 and type 2
Notions of data lake zone layout (raw, curated)
complementaryCompetencies: SQL tuning fundamentals: indexes and query plans
Familiarity with a cloud data warehouse (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift)
Notions of API-based ingestion
indicators: Designs simple schemas with supervision
Maintains model documentation and diagrams
Implements dimensional models under review

coreCompetencies: Designs Kimball dimensional models for several business processes
Applies conformed dimensions and a bus matrix across data marts
Introduces Data Vault (hubs, links, satellites) for the raw vault
Designs lakehouse zone layouts and table formats (Delta, Iceberg)
Defines column-level lineage and metadata capture in the catalog
Evaluates partitioning and clustering strategies for performance
Documents data contracts between producers and consumers
complementaryCompetencies: Notions of streaming ingestion (Kafka, CDC)
Familiarity with orchestration (Airflow, dbt)
Awareness of GDPR classification for sensitive attributes
indicators: Designs end-to-end models for a domain with light review
Contributes to modeling standards and conventions
Leads small-scope architecture tasks

coreCompetencies: Owns the data architecture for a business domain end to end
Chooses between Kimball dimensional modeling and Data Vault by use case
Designs lakehouse architectures with medallion layers and open table formats
Defines data contracts, schemas and versioning between teams
Establishes catalog, lineage and metadata management practices
Models cost and performance trade-offs (storage, compute, TCO)
Guides buy versus build decisions for ingestion and storage tooling
complementaryCompetencies: Real-time and streaming architecture patterns
Feature store design for ML consumption
GDPR-compliant modeling: pseudonymization and retention
indicators: Leads architecture for a domain and mentors junior architects
Defines modeling standards adopted by several teams
Reviews and approves designs from other engineers

coreCompetencies: Designs enterprise data platforms spanning multiple domains
Introduces data mesh principles: domain ownership and data products
Standardizes Data Vault and dimensional patterns across the warehouse
Defines the semantic and metrics layer as a shared contract
Governs catalog, lineage and data contracts at platform scale
Leads buy and build and multi-cloud decisions with documented TCO analysis
Establishes disaster recovery and data retention architecture
complementaryCompetencies: Data virtualization and federation patterns
Privacy-preserving architectures for regulated data
Platform observability and data reliability engineering
indicators: Architects platforms consumed by multiple teams
Defines the technical data architecture roadmap
Leads platform migrations and modernizations

coreCompetencies: Architects the data platform for a business unit or product line
Establishes federated governance and data-product standards across domains
Designs the semantic layer and metrics store as the source of truth
Defines lineage, contracts and catalog strategy at organization scale
Leads FinOps for data: cost allocation, TCO and efficiency programs
Sets architecture for petabyte-scale storage and cross-region replication
Leads and mentors a group of data architects
complementaryCompetencies: Interoperability standards across heterogeneous platforms
Data sharing and marketplace architecture
Sustainable and efficient data platform practices
indicators: Architects platforms for multiple products or domains
Sets long-term architecture strategy for the organization
Resolves cross-team architecture trade-offs

coreCompetencies: Defines the multi-year data architecture strategy for the company
Establishes the operating model for data mesh and domain ownership
Standardizes the enterprise semantic layer, catalog and contracts
Governs buy and build, vendor and multi-cloud strategy with TCO discipline
Sets data governance, privacy and residency architecture across regions
Balances modernization roadmaps against technical debt and cost
Represents data architecture in cross-functional executive planning
complementaryCompetencies: Influences internal architecture standards and reference models
Guides M&A data integration architecture
Mentors principal and staff architects
indicators: Sets corporate data architecture direction
Arbitrates the highest-impact architecture decisions
Recognized internal authority on data architecture

coreCompetencies: Owns the enterprise data architecture vision across all domains
Aligns platform strategy with business and product strategy
Defines reference architectures adopted company-wide
Leads data strategy for mergers, acquisitions and integrations
Establishes governance, contracts and lineage standards at enterprise scale
Sets TCO, FinOps and buy versus build frameworks for data investment
Sponsors data centers of excellence and architecture guilds
complementaryCompetencies: Shapes external technology and vendor relationships
Contributes to open standards and reference models
Advises executives on data platform investment
indicators: Defines data architecture across business units
Influences the direction of the architecture discipline
Principal technical authority for data platforms

coreCompetencies: Sets and executes the enterprise data platform vision and strategy
Aligns data architecture with corporate strategy and P&L
Owns multi-year data platform investment and TCO across the company
Builds and leads the data architecture organization
Governs data privacy, residency and compliance architecture globally
Defines the operating model for domains, contracts and data products
Establishes vendor, multi-cloud and partnership architecture strategy
complementaryCompetencies: Influences industry data architecture practices and standards
Steers strategic technology partnerships
Sponsors architecture talent and succession
indicators: Accountable for enterprise data platform strategy
Executes the long-term data architecture vision
Recognized leader in data architecture
